plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-338 RP10.P08.M04.S20-RP10.P08.M06.S17 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-338 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP10.P08 hermes slice: the M04 secondary workflow tail (next-gate readiness), the full M05 permission and audit binding 22-row Hermes receipt cycle (command matrices, evidence field lists, changed-file/command-result/fixture-summary/blocked-claim/permission-summary/audit-summary/no-real-data receipts, Claude dependency markers, human approval markers, PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics, evidence templates, validation command checks, harness boundary notes, closeout handoffs, regression receipts, next-gate readiness, documentation updates, operator summaries), and the M06 synthetic fixture 17-row head across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H10), Claude review packet (C10), and CP00-339 handoff, without opening Hermes runtime receipts, command runtime execution, object storage, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-338 is a descriptor-only intake-core hermes slice pack (Risk B, 40 units, RP10.P08.M04.S20-RP10.P08.M06.S17) and is supported as claimed. The pack does NOT overclaim production readiness: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both report production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout; production_ready_candidate=true is a local-validation candidate only, with human final approval still required (authority_boundary.human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-339). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are exhaustively attested in contract no_write_attestation, registry/service freezeCp338Result, validators, manifest implementation_summary, and the per-row descriptor flags; no real IO/runtime constructs exist in the changed source (no fs.write, child_process, process.env, require) and every real_client_data_loaded occurrence is a false negation. Claude is held as a read-only reviewer and explicitly not a final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_final_approval_claimed=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), all deep-equal-asserted by scripts/validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs. Hermes control-plane patterns are confined to descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_intake_hermes_slice_no_write; H10 used only for evidence-packet labeling). CP178+ review hardening is encoded (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection refs, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, and summary-only review is correctly prohibited for Risk B. All pre-review validation commands report exit 0; post-review-gated commands (closeout-pack:validate et al.) are correctly deferred until an accepted review result exists, per the documented hardened sequence. Implementation, contract, tests, and validator are mutually consistent (deep-equal contract vs code constants; comprehensive descriptor-only/no-leak/authority test coverage). One trivial, non-blocking classification overlap exists in the review artifact changed-file-scope.json; it does not affect commit behavior since the authoritative manifest worktree policy classifies the pack directory correctly. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. Final enterprise-trust approval remains a human responsibility outside this read-only review.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated-dirty
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-338/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). docs/closeout-packs/cp00-338/ is this pack's own manifest/evidence directory and is therefore related, so listing it under a field named 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' is mildly self-contradictory. This is confined to the auto-generated review-scope artifact; the authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 1717-1725) correctly omits the pack directory, so commit/preserve behavior is unaffected. No production-readiness, boundary, or no-write impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the changed-file-scope generator so a file is not emitted into both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (treat the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ as related). Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-338 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not block pack or goal closeout. The authoritative manifest worktree policy is correct, and the prompt directs the reviewer not to infer unrelated dirty files into pack scope.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-338 intake core hermes slice descriptor pack
